name: PM-KISAN Samman Nidhi
description: Income support of Rs. 6,000 per year in three equal installments to all landholding farmer families.
benefit: Rs. 6,000/year (Rs. 2,000 x 3 installments) directly into bank account via DBT.

eligibility.notes: Landholding farmer family. Excludes institutional landholders and income-tax payers.

name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Gramin
description: Financial assistance to rural BPL households for construction of a pucca house with basic amenities.
benefit: Rs. 1.20 lakh (plain areas) / Rs. 1.30 lakh (hilly/difficult areas) plus MGNREGA labour and toilet support.

eligibility.notes: Houseless / households living in 0, 1 or 2 room kutcha houses as per SECC data.

name: Ayushman Bharat - PM Jan Arogya Yojana (PM-JAY)
description: Health cover of Rs. 5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalisation.
benefit: Rs. 5,00,000 per family per year cashless treatment at empanelled hospitals.

eligibility.notes: Families identified under SECC 2011 deprivation criteria (rural) and occupational categories (urban).

name: Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY)
description: Free LPG connection to women from below-poverty-line households to promote clean cooking fuel.
benefit: Free LPG connection with deposit-free cylinder, first refill and stove support.

eligibility.notes: Adult woman from a BPL household without an existing LPG connection.

name: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana
description: Small savings scheme for the girl child with high interest and tax benefits under Beti Bachao Beti Padhao.
benefit: High fixed interest savings account maturing for the girl's education and marriage; tax-free returns.

eligibility.notes: Account opened by guardian for a girl child below 10 years of age.

name: Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (IGNOAPS)
description: Monthly pension to elderly persons belonging to below-poverty-line households.
benefit: Rs. 200/month (age 60-79) and Rs. 500/month (age 80+), plus state top-up.

eligibility.notes: Aged 60 years or above from a BPL family.

name: Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme (IGNWPS)
description: Monthly pension to widows from below-poverty-line households.
benefit: Rs. 300/month (age 40-79) and Rs. 500/month (age 80+), plus state top-up.

eligibility.notes: Widow aged 40-79 years from a BPL family.

name: Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme (IGNDPS)
description: Monthly pension to persons with severe or multiple disabilities from BPL households.
benefit: Rs. 300/month (age 18-79) and Rs. 500/month (age 80+), plus state top-up.

eligibility.notes: Persons aged 18-79 with 80% or more disability from a BPL family.

name: Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana (PMMVY)
description: Maternity benefit providing partial wage compensation for the first living child.
benefit: Rs. 5,000 in installments for the first child; additional Rs. 6,000 for a second child if a girl.

eligibility.notes: Pregnant and lactating women aged 19+ for the first living child.

name: Post-Matric Scholarship for SC/ST/OBC Students
description: Financial support to SC/ST/OBC students studying at post-matriculation levels.
benefit: Maintenance allowance, reimbursement of tuition and non-refundable fees.

eligibility.notes: Post-matric students from SC/ST/OBC with family income within the ceiling.

name: Pre-Matric Scholarship (Class 9-10)
description: Scholarship to students of classes 9 and 10 from SC/ST/OBC and minority communities.
benefit: Monthly maintenance allowance and annual book grant.

eligibility.notes: Class 9-10 students from reserved categories within the income ceiling.

name: PM Vishwakarma Yojana
description: Support to traditional artisans and craftspeople with recognition, skill upgrade, toolkit and credit.
benefit: Rs. 15,000 toolkit incentive, Rs. 500/day stipend during training, and collateral-free loans up to Rs. 3 lakh.

eligibility.notes: Artisans and craftspeople engaged in one of 18 recognised traditional trades.

name: Pradhan Mantri MUDRA Yojana (PMMY)
description: Collateral-free micro-credit to non-corporate, non-farm small and micro enterprises.
benefit: Loans up to Rs. 10 lakh under Shishu, Kishore and Tarun categories.

eligibility.notes: Any Indian citizen running or planning a small non-farm income-generating activity.

name: Stand-Up India Scheme
description: Bank loans to SC/ST and women entrepreneurs for setting up greenfield enterprises.
benefit: Bank loan between Rs. 10 lakh and Rs. 1 crore for a new manufacturing, services or trading unit.

eligibility.notes: SC/ST or woman entrepreneur setting up a greenfield enterprise (women of any category eligible).

name: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY)
description: Comprehensive crop insurance against yield losses due to natural calamities, pests and diseases.
benefit: Insurance payout for crop loss at low farmer premium (2% Kharif, 1.5% Rabi).

eligibility.notes: Farmers growing notified crops, including sharecroppers and tenant farmers.

name: Kisan Credit Card (KCC)
description: Short-term credit for cultivation and allied activities at subsidised interest.
benefit: Flexible credit limit for crop inputs at 4% effective interest with timely repayment.

eligibility.notes: Farmers, fishers and animal-husbandry households needing working capital.

name: PM Shram Yogi Maandhan (PM-SYM)
description: Voluntary contributory pension scheme for unorganised-sector workers.
benefit: Assured pension of Rs. 3,000/month after age 60 with matching government contribution.

eligibility.notes: Unorganised workers aged 18-40 with monthly income up to Rs. 15,000.

name: Atal Pension Yojana (APY)
description: Guaranteed pension scheme focused on workers in the unorganised sector.
benefit: Guaranteed monthly pension of Rs. 1,000 to Rs. 5,000 after age 60 based on contribution.

eligibility.notes: Any citizen aged 18-40 with a savings bank account (non-taxpayer priority).

name: DAY-NRLM (National Rural Livelihoods Mission)
description: Mobilises rural poor women into self-help groups with access to credit and livelihoods.
benefit: Revolving fund, community investment support and bank linkage for SHG women.

eligibility.notes: Rural women from poor households organised into self-help groups.

name: PM Street Vendor's AtmaNirbhar Nidhi (PM SVANidhi)
description: Working-capital micro-credit for urban street vendors to resume livelihoods.
benefit: Collateral-free working-capital loan up to Rs. 50,000 in tranches with interest subsidy and cashback.

eligibility.notes: Urban street vendors vending on or before 24 March 2020 with a vending certificate.

name: Janani Suraksha Yojana (JSY)
description: Safe-motherhood intervention promoting institutional delivery among poor pregnant women.
benefit: Cash assistance of Rs. 1,400 (rural) / Rs. 1,000 (urban) for institutional delivery.

eligibility.notes: Pregnant women from BPL households opting for institutional delivery.

name: National Family Benefit Scheme (NFBS)
description: One-time lump-sum assistance to BPL households on the death of the primary breadwinner.
benefit: One-time assistance of Rs. 20,000 to the bereaved household.

eligibility.notes: BPL family where the primary breadwinner (aged 18-59) has died.
